name: Chemistry Minor (for Chemical Engineering majors)

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: No requirement groups are parsed (requirementGroups 0), so there is no structured plan, required core or elective rule. The chemistry content within the pool is nonetheless coherent for a chemistry minor: it spans the classical subfields with CHEM 222 (Analytical), CHEM 243 (Organic), CHEM 336/340 (Physical), CHEM 360/361 (Inorganic), CHEM 473/474 (Biochemistry) and CHEM 412/480/491. A significant data caveat applies: this minor's captured course pool is byte-for-byte identical to the Materials Engineering Minor (both scraped from page 580) and both report the same metrics (83 referencedCourses, missingCourseCount 27, deepestPrereqChain 8), so the extraction appears to have attached one shared department course listing to two different minors rather than each minor's own requirements. That shared, undifferentiated pool means the actual minor progression cannot be assessed.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish the chemistry minor's own required/elective structure (e.g., a two-semester organic or physical-chemistry sequence plus curated electives) distinct from the materials minor, and remove non-chemistry courses that belong to the shared department listing.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: The referenced chemistry chains are sensible (deepestPrereqChain 8, e.g., CHEM 125 to 126 to 243 with grade conditions), but prerequisite health is materially hurt by missingCourseCount 27 — an unusually high number of dangling references. The missingCoursesSample (ACCT 200, BME 300, CE 300, CHE 300, CHEM 300, CPT 300, ECE 200/300, EM 600, ENE 200, ENGR 200/211, ENTR 400, FIN 200) is dominated by generic 200-/300-level department placeholders and a graduate EM 600, confirming the pool is a broad scrape rather than a chemistry-minor graph.
dimensions.suggestion: Scope the pool to genuine chemistry-minor courses so the 27 dangling references (most of which are non-chemistry placeholders) fall away, then verify the remaining CHEM chains resolve cleanly.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes appear in the data. For a minor aimed specifically at chemical-engineering majors, the absence is meaningful because it should certify a defined depth of chemistry beyond what the ChemE degree already requires, and no such statement is present.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish 3-5 outcomes describing the added chemistry depth the minor certifies (e.g., advanced organic/physical/analytical competency) and how it goes beyond the ChemE core.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: There is no credit information: statedTotalCredits, summedTermCredits and creditReconcileDelta are all null. Per the rubric this is a data-consistency caveat for a thin-data minor rather than an imbalance finding, but as presented a ChemE major cannot see how many additional chemistry credits the minor requires beyond courses already in their degree.
dimensions.suggestion: State the total credit requirement and, importantly for a minor aimed at ChemE majors, specify which credits are incremental to the ChemE degree versus double-counted.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: The captured page is incomplete: no plan of study, no credit total, no description, and a course pool identical to the Materials Engineering Minor, so a reader cannot tell the two minors apart or determine what the chemistry minor requires.
dimensions.suggestion: Render a distinct chemistry-minor page with its own description, required/elective list and credit total, and fix the parsing so it no longer shares the materials minor's course listing.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: The chemistry courses in the pool cover the enduring, employable subfields (analytical, organic, physical, inorganic, biochemistry), which are relevant and add legitimate depth for a ChemE major. The score is held to adequate because the pool shows no computational-chemistry, cheminformatics or modern-instrumentation content, and its undifferentiated form gives no assurance a student reaches a modern, coherent subset.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a computational-chemistry or cheminformatics elective to the minor's curated list so it reflects current chemical-science practice rather than only classical wet-lab subfields.

descriptionQuality.justification: The sampled course descriptions are present and specific (26 of 26, none thin), with substantive chemistry text (e.g., CHEM 473 Biochemistry, CHEM 336/340 Physical Chemistry, CHEM 243 Organic); only excerpt length is truncated. The score is held below 5 because the pool includes many non-chemistry courses whose presence here is a scoping artifact rather than a description-quality issue.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: While individual CHEM prerequisites are stated with grade conditions, prerequisite clarity is undermined by 27 dangling references — largely non-chemistry 200-/300-level placeholders (BME 300, CE 300, ECE 300, EM 600, ENTR 400, FIN 200) — that make the minor's actual entry requirements ambiguous.

missingTopics.topic: A defined incremental chemistry core for ChemE majors
missingTopics.rationale: Because the minor targets ChemE students who already take CHEM 125/126/243, the key missing element is a stated set of additional courses (e.g., CHEM 336/340 Physical, CHEM 360 Inorganic, CHEM 473 Biochemistry) that add genuine new depth.
The pool's physical-chemistry courses (CHEM 336/340) are the natural anchor for a computational/quantum-chemistry elective, which is absent yet central to modern chemical research.
CHEM 222 Analytical appears, but there is no advanced instrumental-analysis/spectroscopy course to give the minor contemporary characterization skills.
No course connects chemistry to data analysis; a cheminformatics elective would modernize a minor otherwise built on classical subfields.

missingTopics.topic: Descoping non-chemistry courses from the pool
missingTopics.rationale: The listing carries BME, CE, ENGR, CS, ACCT/FIN and management placeholders shared with the materials minor; removing them is prerequisite to a coherent chemistry minor.

emergingTrends.rationale: Machine-learning-driven molecular property prediction is reshaping chemistry; building on CHEM 336/340 physical chemistry would position the minor for it.
A green-chemistry elective linked to CHEM 243 organic would align the minor with sustainability priorities relevant to ChemE careers.

emergingTrends.trend: Chemical data science and high-throughput experimentation
emergingTrends.rationale: Coupling analytical chemistry (CHEM 222) with data methods reflects the automation and data-rich direction of modern chemical laboratories.

emergingTrends.trend: Biochemistry and chemical biology at the interface
emergingTrends.rationale: CHEM 473/474 Biochemistry is a strong anchor to emphasize the chemistry-biology interface increasingly central to pharma and materials work.
An electrochemistry elective would connect the minor to battery/energy-storage chemistry, a fast-growing area adjacent to chemical engineering.

topSuggestions: Fix the data-extraction conflation: this minor's course pool is identical to the Materials Engineering Minor (both from page 580) and both report the same 27 dangling references — give each minor its own scraped requirements.
Publish the chemistry minor's actual required/elective structure and total credits (all currently null), specifying which credits are incremental to the ChemE degree.
Scope out the non-chemistry placeholder courses (BME/CE/ECE/ENGR/ACCT/FIN/EM 600) so the 27 dangling references resolve and the CHEM chains stand alone.
Publish 3-5 learning outcomes describing the added chemistry depth the minor certifies beyond the ChemE core.
Add a computational-chemistry or cheminformatics elective so the curated minor reflects current practice, not only classical subfields.
